title: Implement URL Shortener REST API in Go with In-Memory Storage

steps.description: Define domain entities for URL and short link mapping.
steps.done_condition: Domain entities file created with tests.

steps.description: Create port interfaces for URL shortening and retrieval.
steps.done_condition: Port interfaces file created with tests.

steps.description: Implement in-memory storage logic for URLs.
Build REST API endpoints for URL shortening and retrieval.
steps.done_condition: REST API endpoints created and tested.

steps.description: Implement use cases for URL shortening and retrieval.
Setup the composition root to wire up the layers.
Develop integration tests for the full URL shortener API.

mergeOrder: Finally, merge composition root and run integration tests.

riskRegister.mitigation: Plan for future persistence storage implementation.
riskRegister.risk: Data will be lost on application restarts due to in-memory storage.

riskRegister.mitigation: Consider architectural refactoring in the future.
riskRegister.risk: Scalability limitations due to single-binary constraints.

successCriteria: URL shortening and retrieval functionalities work as intended.
All tests pass for domain, ports, adapters, use cases, and integration.
API documentation is created and accessible.
